title: Yann LeCun's Home Page

| |  | | --- | |  | |  | | HOME | | [BIO](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1liH9utlyETxvywBQAaXWRcK-l-qV9SCFTEVG0QJLktI/edit?usp=sharing) | | PUBLICATIONS | | [arXiv Papers](http://arxiv.org/find/all/1/all:+AND+yann+lecun/0/1/0/all/0/1) | | [G-Scholar Profile](http://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=WLN3QrAAAAAJ&pagesize=100&view_op=list_works) | | SOFTWARE | | [CBLL](http://www.cs.nyu.edu/~yann/index.html) | | RESEARCH | | [TEACHING](http://www.cs.nyu.edu/~yann/courses.html) | | WHAT's NEW | |  | | DjVu | | LENET | | MNIST OCR DATA | | [NORB DATASET](http://www.cs.nyu.edu/~ylclab/data/norb-v1.0/index.html) | |  | | MUSIC | | PHOTOS | | HOBBIES | | FUN STUFF | |  | | LINKS | |  | | [CILVR](http://cilvr.nyu.edu) | | [CDS](http://cds.nyu.edu) | | [CS Dept](http://www.cs.nyu.edu) | | [Courant](http://www.cims.nyu.edu) | | [NYU](http://www.nyu.edu) | | [Lush](http://lush.sourceforge.net) | | [DjVu Plugin for Win/Mac](http://www.djvu.com/download.html) | | [DjVu Plugin for Unix](http://djvu.sourceforge.net) | |  | | Websites that I maintain | | [Lush](http://lush.sourceforge.net) | | [NIPS Online](http://nips.djvuzone.org) | | [DjVuZone](http://www.djvuzone.org) | | [Any2DjVu](http://any2djvu.djvuzone.org) | | [Bib2Web](http://bib2web.djvuzone.org) | | [DjVuLibre](http://djvu.sourceforge.net) | |  | |  | |  | |  | |  | | Yann LeCun's Home Page  [**Yann LeCun**](http://yann.lecun.com),   Executive Chairman, Advanced Machine Intelligence (AMI Labs)  Jacob T. Schwartz Professor of Computer Science, Data Science, Neural Science, and Electrical and Computer Engineering,  [Courant Institute - School of Mathematics, Computing, and Data Science](https://cims.nyu.edu/), [New York University](http://www.nyu.edu).  ACM Turing Award Laureate, (sounds like I'm bragging, but a condition of accepting the award is to write this next to your name)  Member, [National Academy of Engineering](https://www.nae.edu/), [National Academy of Sciences](https://www.nasonline.org/), [Académie des Sciences](https://www.academie-sciences.fr/en/)  Fellow, [ACM](https://www.acm.org/), [AAAI](https://aaai.org/), [AAAS](https://www.aaas.org/), [SIF](https://www.socinfo.fr/)  last updated: 2025-05-26     |  | | --- | | **Social Networks** |   [Threads/Fediverse: @yannlecun](https://www.threads.net/@yannlecun) (ML/AI, announcements, photos, politics)  [LinkedIn: yann-lecun](https://www.linkedin.com/in/yann-lecun/) (ML/AI research and industry, announcements)  [Facebook: yann.lecun](https://www.facebook.com/yann.lecun) (general, science, ML/AI, culture, hobbies, photos)  [BlueSky: @yann-lecun.bsky.social](https://bsky.app/profile/yann-lecun.bsky.social) (Not using it much)  [Twitter/X: @ylecun](https://twitter.com/ylecun) (I no longer write posts on X)  Note: X has devolved into an antagonistic propaganda tool. As of December 2024, I no longer write posts on X. As a favor to my numerous followers, I tweet links to posts on other platforms (occasionally), I retweet interesting contents from others (sometimes), and I comment on tweets by friends (rarely). But I don't write substantial content.     |  | | --- | | **Biography / CV** |   [Curriculum Vitae](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mtBSxZgie86MAn1Mgoowj92hAwo8wIbCCvY9wwKih2Q/edit?usp=sharing)  [bios of various lengths in English and en francais](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1liH9utlyETxvywBQAaXWRcK-l-qV9SCFTEVG0QJLktI/edit?usp=sharing)     |  | | --- | | **Contact Information** |   **NYU Affiliations:**  [CILVR Lab](https://wp.nyu.edu/cilvr/) (Computational Intelligence, Learning, Vision, Robotics)  [Computer Science Department](http://www.cs.nyu.edu)  [Center for Data Science](http://cds.nyu.edu/), NYU  [Courant Institute - School of Mathematics, Computing, and Data Science](http://www.cims.nyu.edu)  [Center for Neural Science](http://www.cns.nyu.edu/), Faculty of Arts and Sciences  [Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering](http://engineering.nyu.edu/academics/departments/electrical), NYU Tandon School of Engineering  [New York University](http://nyu.edu)  **Assistants**  Executive Assistant - AMI Labs: **Sean Nguyen**: sean[at]amilabs.xyz  Administrative Aide - NYU: Hong Tam +1-212-998-3374     hongtam[at]cs.nyu.edu    **FOR INVITATIONS TO SPEAK**: please send email to **lecuninvites[at]gmail.com**  (I really can't handle invitations sent to other email addresses)  **IF YOU REALLY NEED ME TO DO SOMETHING FOR YOU**: (e.g. a review, a letter...) please send email to Sean Nguyen **sean[at]amilabs.xyz**  **NYU coordinates:**  Address: Room 516, [60 Fifth Avenue, New York,](https://goo.gl/maps/oFEVEAvHsTBzHrVZ9) NY 10011, USA.  Email: yann.lecun[at]nyu.edu (I may not respond right away)  Phone: +1-212-998-3283 (I am very unlikely to respond or listen to voice mail in a timely manner)  **AMI Labs Coordinates:**  Email: yann[at]amilabs.xyz (I may not respond right away)     |  | | --- | | **Publications, Talks, Courses, Videos** |   **Main Research Interests**:  AI, Machine Learning, Computer Vision, Robotics, and Computational Neuroscience. I am also interested Physics of Computation, and many applications of machine learning.  **Publications**:  [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=WLN3QrAAAAAJ&hl=fr)  [Papers on OpenReview.net](https://openreview.net/profile?id=~Yann_LeCun1)  [Preprints on ArXiv](http://arxiv.org/find/all/1/all:+AND+yann+lecun/0/1/0/all/0/1)  Publications up to 2014 with PDF and DjVu  **Talks / Slide Decks**:  [Slides of (most of) my talks](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/0BxKBnD5y2M8NUXhZaXBCNXE4QlE?resourcekey=0-WtYv0wV-8DFNsFWfRUcpsw&usp=sharing)  **Deep Learning Course**:  Deep Learning course at NYU:   Complete course on Deep Learning, with all the material available on line including lectures and practicums, videos, slide decks, homeworks, Jupyter notebooks, and transcripts in several languages.  **Videos:** Playlists on YouTube:   * [Talks by Yann LeCun](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL80I41oVxglK--is17UhoHVosOLFEJzKQ) * [Lectures Series by Yann LeCun](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL80I41oVxglJ0kTDV7i3aHBIXe65nTxE7) * [Debates and Panels with Yann LeCun](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL80I41oVxglJ2TJzOJtnPZthD5mj8kfZX) * [Interviews of Yann LeCun](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL80I41oVxglJOokgsaIKw4ZJORQC0Qc_5) * [Demos by Yann LeCun](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL80I41oVxglKKxF1OBbKHdOEX2VZVNzAR) * [Six short videos to explain AI, Machine Learning, Deep Learning and Convolutional Nets (2016)](https://code.fb.com/ai-research/ai-revealed/)  [Talks on VideoLectures:](https://videolectures.net/lecturer/6533/?sort=lectures_most_viewed) (from 2007 to 2016).    |  | | --- | | **Working Paper** |      [A Path Towards Autonomous Machine Intelligence](https://openreview.net/forum?id=BZ5a1r-kVsf)(June 2022) How could machines learn as efficiently as humans and animals? How could machines learn to reason and plan? How could machines learn representations of percepts and action plans at multiple levels of abstraction, enabling them to reason, predict, and plan at multiple time horizons? This position paper proposes an architecture and training paradigms with which to construct autonomous intelligent agents. It combines concepts such as configurable predictive world model, behavior driven through intrinsic motivation, and hierarchical joint embedding architectures trained with self-supervised learning.  **Recent lectures on the topic:**   * 2025-09-16: Self-Supervised Learning, JEA, World Models, and the Future of AI¨    Harvard Center for Mathematical Sciences and Applications   [Video on YouTube](https://youtu.be/yUmDRxV0krg?si=YqXiGqZaeAMHk6bB) * 2025-04-27: "Shaping the Future of AI"    Distinguished Lecture at National University of Singapore University.    [Video on YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/live/m3H2q6MXAzs?si=d8X1pAITkF85wtfq)    [Slide deck](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1j3A8SYtkJqui7CotCMuvES2LpR17JYrQ/view?usp=drive_link) * 2024-10-18: "How could machines reach human-level intelligence?"    Distinguished Lecture at Columbia University    [Video on Youtube](https://youtu.be/xL6Y0dpXEwc?si=DIfge-gfKGGcJo2F)    [Slide deck](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1F0Q8Fq0h2pHq9j6QIbzqhBCfTXJ7Vmf4/view?usp=drive_link)  |  | | --- | | **Books** |      [Quand La Machine Apprend](https://www.odilejacob.fr/catalogue/sciences/informatique/quand-la-machine-apprend_9782738149312.php)La revolution des neurones artificiels et de l'apprentissage profond (Editions Odile Jacob, Octobre 2019)  Exists in Chinese, Japanese, and Russian.  [La Plus Belle Histoire de l'Intelligence](https://www.lisez.com/livre-grand-format/la-plus-belle-histoire-de-lintelligence/9782221221105)Des origines aux neurones artificiels : vers une nouvelle étape de l'évolution  Stanislas Dehaene, Yann Le Cun, Jacques Girardon (Éditions Robert Laffont, Octobre 2018)    |  | | --- | | **[Pamphlets and opinions](http://www.cs.nyu.edu/~yann/pamphlets/)** |       [How to Build a Vibrant Technology Industry (by attracting scientists to your country)](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1LlNCY9_4MXhilnbu9MqKhSjHjMGqtDA8zZmSQynVjmY) (2025-05-30)As the US seems set on self-sabotaging its extraordinarily successful system of public research funding, countries in Europe and elsewhere may want to seize the opportunity to reboot their own research ecosystem and jumpstart their technology industry.  [Five Ways to Act Deluded, Stupid, Ineffective, or Evil](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1lz8PaTIXrfRsQtbWE0ta_qrpjZi6GUAErwJmmkBay2Y) (2025-04-28)Using ideas from AI research to explain the failure modes of human behavior, with examples from international trade policy.  Comments on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/yann-lecun_five-ways-to-act-deluded-stupid-ineffective-activity-7327058733967052800-TsUK), [Threads](https://www.threads.com/@yannlecun/post/DJfEd4xu7qm?xmt=AQF07t11NIYSTE2B7oaN7-_q58_tr6WrMkzkA3Q_kIii1Q), [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1F4gLnjBtY/)[AI and the Future of Europe, a Defining Moment](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1cEdrzje8qN9cRx3DVfIqXGO5dQ3Hn4uV7GnylqmRyhc/edit?tab=t.0=h.k1p5gwg20jt1) (2025-02-09)by Bernhard Schölkopf, Nuria Oliver, and Yann LeCun. In which we argue that funding from the newly-formed European AI Research Council should go to small groups of talented researchers and not be administered as a large project managed from the top down.  This opinion piece was published in January 2025 simultaneously in [Les Échos](https://www.lesechos.fr/idees-debats/cercle/opinion-lia-et-lavenir-de-leurope-un-moment-decisif-2147630), [Handelsbaltt](https://www.handelsblatt.com/meinung/gastbeitraege/gastkommentar-im-ki-zeitalter-ist-das-wertvollste-kapital-der-mensch-01/100106696.html), and [El Pais](https://elpais.com/opinion/2025-02-10/la-inteligencia-artificial-y-el-futuro-de-europa-un-momento-decisivo.html).   [Address to the UN Security Council](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Yz3-Xq3JJEoPGdgeDdT6Ma-j3iI66zOfVsB0OsQZMqY/edit?usp=sharing) (2024-12-19) I was invited by Secretary of State Antony Blinken to speak about AI at the UN Security Council meeting on 2024-12-19. I was followed by Fei-Fei Li, and representatives from UNSC member states.  In [my speech](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Yz3-Xq3JJEoPGdgeDdT6Ma-j3iI66zOfVsB0OsQZMqY/edit?usp=sharing), I argued for free/open source foundation models and for international cooperation to train "universal" foundation models that speak all the languages in the world and understand all cultures and value systems.  My speech starts at the 00:12:20 mark in [this video](https://www.c-span.org/program/united-nations/secretary-blinken-chairs-un-security-council-on-artificial-intelligence/653448)   Proposal for a new publishing model in Computer Science (2009-12-01) Many computer Science researchers are complaining that our emphasis on highly selective conference publications, and our double-blind reviewing system stifles innovation and slow the rate of progress of Science and technology.  This pamphlet proposes a new publishing model based on an open repository and open (but anonymous) reviews which creates a "market" between papers and reviewing entities.     |  | | --- | | **Students synthesis and composition]    Former PhD Students  * [Jiachen Zhu](https://jiachenzhu.github.io/) (2025 NYU CS) [SSL and optimization], Skild AI. * [Vlad Sobal](https://vladisai.github.io/) (2025, NYU Rives](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=vqb78-gAAAAJ&hl=en) (2024 NYU CS) [Protein design] FAIR, CEO Evolutionary Scale, MIT EECS & Broad Institute. * [Katrina Drozdov Gustave Eiffel with Laurent Najman) [dedicated hardware for ConvNets, vision, Torch-7] Twitter, Nvidia, VP of Research DeepMind * [Fu Jie Huang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fu-jie-huang-2503295/) (2013 NYU CS) [DL for vision] Milabra, Kanerai * [Kevin Jarrett](https://www.linkedin.com/in/kevin-jarrett-ph-d-94262149/) (2012 NYU Neural Science) [DL models of biological vision] Bridgewater,...,Barclays * Matthew Grimes (2012 NYU) [SLAM] Cambridge, DeepMind * [Y-Lan Boureau](https://y-lanb.org/) (2012, NYU-INRIA with Jean Ponce) [sparse feature learning for vision] Flatiron Institute, FAIR, CEO ThrivePal) * [Koray Kavukcuoglu](https://koray.kavukcuoglu.org/) (2010, NYU) [sparse auto-encoders for unsupervised feature learning] NEC Labs, VP of GenAI DeepMind, CTO DeepMind, Chief AI Architect Google. * [Piotr Mirowski](https://piotrmirowski.com/) (2010 NYU) Bell Labs, Microsoft, DeepMind * [Ayse Naz Erkan](https://www.linkedin.com/in/nazerkan/) (2010 NYU, with Yasemine Altun) Twitter, Robinhood, CEO Laminar AI. * [Marc'Aurelio Ranzato](https://ranzato.github.io/) (2009 NYU) Google X-Labs, FAIR, DeepMind. * [Sumit Chopra](https://spchopra.org/) (2008 NYU) AT&T Labs-Research, FAIR, Imagen, faculty NYU. * [Raia Hadsell](https://raiahadsell.com/index.html) (2008 NYU) SRI, VP Foundations DeepMind * [Feng Ning](https://www.linkedin.com/in/feng-ning-08804812/) (2006 NYU) Bank of America, Société Générale, ScotiaBank, AQR Capital, VP AllianceBernstein.    Former Postdocs  * [Ravid Schwartz-Ziv](https://www.ravid-shwartz-ziv.com/) (NYU, 2023-2025) Meta-FAIR * [Amir Bar](https://www.amirbar.net/) (FAIR, 2024-2025), Meta-FAIR * [Micah Goldblum](https://goldblum.github.io/) (NYU 2021-2024), Columbia University * [Grégoire Mialon](https://gregoiremialon.github.io/) (FAIR 2021-2023), Meta-GenAI * [Randall Balestriero](https://randallbalestriero.github.io/) (FAIR 2021-2023), Brown University * [Nicolas Carion](https://www.nicolascarion.com/) (NYU 2020-2022), FAIR * [Yubei Chen](https://yubeichen.com/) (FAIR 2020-2022), UC Davis * [Li Jing](http://jingli.io/) (FAIR 2019-2021), OpenAI * [Jacob Browning](https://www.jacob-browning.com/) (NYU 2019-2023): philosophy and history of AI (Berggruen Transformation of the Human program) * [Phillip Schmitt](https://philippschmitt.com/) (NYU 2019-2021): AI and the visual arts (Berggruen Transformation of the Human program) * [Stéphane Deny](https://sites.google.com/view/stephanedeny/home) (FAIR 2019-2021), U of Aalto * [Alfredo Canziani](https://atcold.github.io/) (NYU 2017-2022), NYU: autonomous driving, AI education * [Behnam Neyshabur](https://www.neyshabur.net/) (NYU 20172019), Google, DeepMind: deep learning landscape, self-supervised learning * [Jure Zbontar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=krWNpKoAAAAJ) (NYU 2016-2017). FAIR, OpenAI: temporal prediction * [Anna Choromanska](https://engineering.nyu.edu/faculty/anna-choromanska) (NYU 2014-2017) NYU Tandon: applied mathematics * [Pablo Sprechmann](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=YCPycGAAAAAJ) (NYU 2014-2017), DeepMind: applied mathematics and signal processing * [Joan Bruna](https://cims.nyu.edu/~bruna/) (NYU 2012-2014), FAIR, UC Berkeley, NYU: applied mathematics * [Camille Couprie](https://perso.esiee.fr/~coupriec/) (NYU 2011-2013), FAIR: computer vision * [Tom Schaul](https://schaul.site44.com/) (NYU 2011-2013), DeepMind: machine learning and optimization * [Jason Rolfe](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jason-rolfe-323b231/) (NYU 2011-2013), D-Wave, Variational AI: computational neuroscience * [Leo Zhu](https://www.yitutech.com/en/about-us) (NYU 2010-2011), CEO Yitu: hierarchical vision models. * [Arthur Szlam](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=u3-FxUgAAAAJ) (NYU 2009-2011), CUNY, FAIR, DeepMind: applied mathematics. * Karol Gregor (NYU 2008-2011), Janelia Farm, DeepMind: machine learning. * [Trivikraman Thampy](https://www.linkedin.com/in/trivikraman-thampy/) (NYU 2008-2009), CEO Play Games24x7: financial modeling and prediction. * [Joseph Turian](https://www.linkedin.com/in/turian/) (NYU 2007-2007), Founder MetaOptimize: energy-based models. * [Margarita Osadchy](https://rita.osadchy.net/) (NEC Labs 2002-2003), University of Haifa: energy-based models, face detection with ConvNets. * Yoshua Bengio (AT&T Bell Labs 1992-1993), MILA - Université de Montréal. * Patrice Simard (AT&T Bell Labs 1991-1992), AT&T Labs, Microsoft Research.  |  | | --- | | **Bragging Zone** |    Honors and Awards  * Inaugural Trailblazer Award, the New York Academy of Sciences, 2025, [link] * **Queen Elizabeth Prize for Engineering**, 2025 (shared with Yoshua Bengio, Geoffrey Hinton, John Hopfield (Foundations), Bill Dally, Jensen Huang (Hardware), Fei-Fei Li (Data). [[link]](https://qeprize.org/winners/dr-yann-lecun) * AMS Josiah Willard Gibbs Lecturer, JMM Seattle, 2025, [[link]](https://www.ams.org/meetings/lectures/meet-gibbs-lect) * **VinFuture Grand Prize**, 2024 (shared with Yoshua Bengio, Geoff Hinton, Jensen Huang, Fei-Fei Li), [[link]](https://vinfutureprize.org/laureates/professor-yann-lecun/), [[acceptance speech]](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/yann-lecun_my-acceptance-speech-at-the-vinfuture-prize-activity-7271825093238951936-Bitt?utm_source=share), [[pictures]](https://photos.app.goo.gl/RxWfquafocGbqXyk6) * Trailblazer in Science Award, New York Hall of Science, 2024, [[link]](https://nysci.org/gala) * Doctorate Honoris Causa, Université de Genève, 2024, [[link]](https://www.unige.ch/dies/ceremonie-2024), [[lecture]](https://youtu.be/eDY9FUT5ces?si=yPsuZFiYVPhbp4yT) * Professor Honoris Causa, ESIEE / Université Gustave Eiffel, 2024, [[link]](https://www.esiee.fr/actualites/esiee-paris-remet-a-yann-le-cun-le-titre-de-professeur-honoris-causa) * Lifetime Honorary Membership, New York Academy of Sciences, 2024,
